00038 package java.io;
00039 
00040 /* Written using "Java Class Libraries", 2nd edition, ISBN 0-201-31002-3
00041  * "The Java Language Specification", ISBN 0-201-63451-1
00042  * plus online API docs for JDK 1.2 beta from http://www.javasoft.com.
00043  * Status:  Believed complete and correct.
00044  */
00045  
00057 public class DataInputStream extends InputStream implements DataInput
00058 {
00059     // Byte buffer, used to make primitive read calls more efficient.
00060     private byte[] buf = new byte [8];
00061   
00065     protected InputStream in;
00066   
00073     public DataInputStream(InputStream in)
00074     {
00075         this.in = in;
00076     }
00077 
00084     public int read() throws IOException
00085     {
00086         return in.read();
00087     }
00088 
00102     public final int read(byte[] b) throws IOException
00103     {
00104         return in.read(b, 0, b.length);
00105     }
00106 
00123     public final int read(byte[] b, int off, int len) throws IOException
00124     {
00125         return in.read (b, off, len);
00126     }
00127 
00142     public final void readFully(byte[] b) throws EOFException, IOException
00143     {
00144         readFully (b, 0, b.length);
00145     }
00146 
00166     public final void readFully(byte[] buf, int offset, int len)
00167         throws EOFException, IOException
00168     {
00169         if (len < 0)
00170             throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Negative length: " + len);
00171     
00172         while (len > 0)
00173         {
00174             // in.read will block until some data is available.
00175             int numread = in.read (buf, offset, len);
00176             
00177             if (numread < 0)
00178                 throw new EOFException();
00179             
00180             len -= numread;
00181             offset += numread;
00182         }
00183     }
00184 
00197     public int skipBytes(int n) throws IOException
00198     {
00199         if (n <= 0)
00200             return 0;    
00201     
00202         try
00203         {
00204             return (int) in.skip(n);
00205         }
00206         catch (EOFException x)
00207         {
00208             // do nothing.
00209         }         
00210     
00211         return n;
00212     }
00213 
00232     public final boolean readBoolean() throws IOException
00233     {
00234         return convertToBoolean(in.read());
00235     }
00236 
00252     public final byte readByte() throws IOException
00253     {
00254         return convertToByte(in.read());
00255     }
00256 
00273     public final int readUnsignedByte() throws IOException
00274     {
00275         return convertToUnsignedByte(in.read());
00276     }
00277 
00305     public final short readShort() throws IOException
00306     {
00307         readFully(buf, 0, 2);
00308     
00309         return convertToShort(buf);
00310     }
00311 
00339     public final int readUnsignedShort() throws IOException
00340     {
00341         readFully(buf, 0, 2);
00342     
00343         return convertToUnsignedShort(buf);
00344     }
00345 
00371     public final char readChar() throws IOException
00372     {
00373         readFully(buf, 0, 2);
00374 
00375         return convertToChar(buf);
00376     }
00377 
00405     public final int readInt() throws IOException
00406     {
00407         readFully(buf, 0, 4);
00408     
00409         return convertToInt(buf);
00410     }
00411 
00443     public final long readLong() throws IOException
00444     {
00445         readFully(buf, 0, 8);
00446     
00447         return convertToLong(buf);
00448     }
00449 
00470     public final float readFloat() throws IOException
00471     {
00472         return Float.intBitsToFloat(readInt());
00473     }
00474 
00496     public final double readDouble () throws IOException
00497     {
00498         return Double.longBitsToDouble (readLong ());
00499     }
00500 
00572     public final String readUTF() throws IOException
00573     {
00574         return readUTF(this);
00575     }
00576 
00589     public static final String readUTF(DataInput in) throws IOException
00590     {
00591         final int UTFlen = in.readUnsignedShort ();
00592     
00593         byte[] buf = new byte [UTFlen];
00594 
00595         // This blocks until the entire string is available rather than
00596         // doing partial processing on the bytes that are available and then
00597         // blocking.  An advantage of the latter is that Exceptions
00598         // could be thrown earlier.  The former is a bit cleaner.
00599         in.readFully (buf, 0, UTFlen);
00600 
00601         return convertFromUTF (buf);
00602     }
00603     
00611     public long skip(long n) throws IOException
00612     {
00613         return in.skip(n);
00614     }
00615     
00622     public int available() throws IOException
00623     {
00624         return in.available();
00625     }
00626 
00632     public void close() throws IOException
00633     {
00634         in.close();
00635     }
00636 
00643     public synchronized void mark(int limit)
00644     {
00645         in.mark(limit);
00646     }
00647 
00653     public synchronized void reset() throws IOException
00654     {
00655         in.reset();
00656     }
00657     
00662     public boolean markSupported()
00663     {
00664         return in.markSupported();
00665     }
00666 
00667     private static byte convertToByte(int i) throws IOException
00668     {
00669         if (i < 0)
00670             throw new IOException();
00671     
00672         return (byte) i;
00673     }
00674 
00675     private static boolean convertToBoolean(int b) throws IOException
00676     {
00677         if (b < 0)
00678             throw new IOException ();
00679     
00680         return (b != 0);
00681     }
00682 
00683     private static int convertToUnsignedByte(int i) throws IOException
00684     {
00685         if (i < 0)
00686             throw new IOException();
00687     
00688         return (i & 0xFF);
00689     }
00690 
00691     private static short convertToShort(byte[] buf)
00692     {
00693         return (short) ((buf [0] << 8) | (buf [1] & 0xff));  
00694     }  
00695 
00696     private static int convertToUnsignedShort(byte[] buf)
00697     {
00698         return (((buf [0] & 0xff) << 8) | (buf [1] & 0xff));  
00699     }
00700 
00701     private static char convertToChar(byte[] buf)
00702     {
00703         return (char) ((buf [0] << 8) | (buf [1] & 0xff));  
00704     }  
00705 
00706     private static int convertToInt(byte[] buf)
00707     {
00708         return (((buf [0] & 0xff) << 24)
00709             | ((buf [1] & 0xff) << 16)
00710             | ((buf [2] & 0xff) << 8)
00711             | (buf [3] & 0xff));  
00712     }
00713 
00714     private static long convertToLong(byte[] buf)
00715     {
00716         return (((long)(buf [0] & 0xff) << 56) |
00717             ((long)(buf [1] & 0xff) << 48) |
00718             ((long)(buf [2] & 0xff) << 40) |
00719             ((long)(buf [3] & 0xff) << 32) |
00720             ((long)(buf [4] & 0xff) << 24) |
00721             ((long)(buf [5] & 0xff) << 16) |
00722             ((long)(buf [6] & 0xff) <<  8) |
00723             ((long)(buf [7] & 0xff)));  
00724     }
00725 
00726     // FIXME: This method should be re-thought.  I suspect we have multiple
00727     // UTF-8 decoders floating around.  We should use the standard charset
00728     // converters, maybe and adding a direct call into one of the new
00729     // NIO converters for a super-fast UTF8 decode.
00730     private static String convertFromUTF(byte[] buf)
00731         throws EOFException, UTFDataFormatException
00732     {
00733         // Give StringBuffer an initial estimated size to avoid 
00734         // enlarge buffer frequently
00735         StringBuffer strbuf = new StringBuffer (buf.length / 2 + 2);
00736 
00737         for (int i = 0; i < buf.length; )
00738         {
00739              if ((buf [i] & 0x80) == 0) // bit pattern 0xxxxxxx
00740                  strbuf.append ((char) (buf [i++] & 0xFF));
00741              else if ((buf [i] & 0xE0) == 0xC0) // bit pattern 110xxxxx
00742              {
00743                  if (i + 1 >= buf.length || (buf [i + 1] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
00744                      throw new UTFDataFormatException ();
00745 
00746                  strbuf.append((char) (((buf [i++] & 0x1F) << 6)
00747                     | (buf [i++] & 0x3F)));
00748              }
00749              else if ((buf [i] & 0xF0) == 0xE0) // bit pattern 1110xxxx
00750              {
00751                  if (i + 2 >= buf.length
00752                     || (buf [i + 1] & 0xC0) != 0x80
00753                     || (buf [i + 2] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
00754                  {
00755                     throw new UTFDataFormatException ();
00756                  }
00757 
00758                  strbuf.append((char) (((buf [i++] & 0x0F) << 12)
00759                     | ((buf [i++] & 0x3F) << 6)
00760                     | (buf [i++] & 0x3F)));
00761              }
00762              else
00763              {
00764                  // must be ((buf [i] & 0xF0) == 0xF0 || (buf [i] & 0xC0) == 0x80)
00765                  // bit patterns 1111xxxx or 10xxxxxx
00766                  throw new UTFDataFormatException ();
00767              }
00768         }
00769 
00770         return strbuf.toString();
00771     }
00772 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:23 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
